```](#)# Creating your file...This may take a few minutes. Thanks for your patience.CancelYour file is readyOKCreate protection groups to manage the backup operations for a set of KVM resources. A protection group is a logical grouping of resources such as VMs and storage pools that you want to protect together. You need to create a protection group to back up KVM virtual machines or storage pools.You can perform the following tasks related to protection groups:* Create a protection group.
* View protection details.
* Back up a protection group now. See [Back up KVM workloads now](br-use-kvm-backup.html).
* Edit a protection group.
* Delete a protection group.## Create a protection groupGroup VMs and storage pools that you want to protect together into a protection group.**Required Console role**
Backup and Recovery super admin or Backup and Recovery backup admin role. [Learn about NetApp Console access roles for all services](https://docs.netapp.com/us-en/console-setup-admin/reference-iam-predefined-roles.html).Steps1. From the NetApp Backup and Recovery menu, select **Inventory**.
2. Select a workload to view the protection details.
3. Select the Actions icon ![Actions option](./media/icon-action.png) > **View details**.
4. Select the **Protection groups** tab.
5. Select **Create protection group**.
6. Provide a name for the protection group.
7. Select a host to list the available VMs on that host. To include VMs from all discovered hosts, select **All** in the hosts list.
8. Select individual VMs that you want to include in the protection group, or select **All** to include all VMs in the list.VMs that you select appear in the **Added virtual machines** area. You can remove individual VMs from the list or empty the list entirely. You can add VMs from multiple KVM hosts to the list.
9. Select **Next**.
10. Select the protection policy that you want to apply to the protection group, or create a new policy by selecting **Create new policy**.For more information about creating a backup policy, refer to [Create and manage policies](br-use-policies-create.html).
11. Select **Next**.
12. Review the configuration.
13. Select **Create** to create the protection group.## Edit a protection groupEdit a protection group if you need to change details of the protection group without deleting and recreating it.Steps1. From the NetApp Backup and Recovery menu, select **Inventory**.
2. Select a workload to view the protection details.
3. Select the Actions icon ![Actions option](./media/icon-action.png) > **View details**.
4. Select the **Protection groups** tab.
5. Select the protection group that you want to edit.
6. Select the Actions icon ![Actions option](./media/icon-action.png) > **Edit**.
7. Make any needed changes to the general details and VMs in the protection group.
8. Select **Next**.
9. If needed, change the policy associated with the protection group.
10. Select **Next**.
11. Review the configuration and select **Submit**.## Delete a protection groupDeleting a protection group removes it and all associated backup schedules. You might want to delete a protection group if it is no longer needed.Steps1. From the NetApp Backup and Recovery menu, select **Inventory**.
2. Select a workload to view the protection details.
3. Select the Actions icon ![Actions option](./media/icon-action.png) > **View details**.
4. Select the **Protection groups** tab.
5. Select the protection group that you want to delete.
6. Select the Actions icon ![Actions option](./media/icon-action.png) > **Delete**.
7. Review the confirmation message about deleting the associated backups and confirm the deletion.
